nodes.evidence: Obesity is a metabolic disease that is the result of excessive body fat accumulation, negatively impacting health by increasing the risk of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and osteoarthritis [1,2]. Obesity is a global epidemic with an estimated prevalence of 40% among the world population, and it has been linked to the development of chronic inflammation [2]. Likewise, pro-inflammatory cytokine production is increased in animal models of high-fat diet (HFD)-induced obesity [3,4].
Obesity is a metabolic disease resulting from excess body fat accumulation associated with chronic systemic inflammation.
Likewise, pro-inflammatory cytokine production is increased in animal models of high-fat diet (HFD)-induced obesity [3,4].

Additionally, we showed that leptin, a hormone derived from adipose tissue, was significantly upregulated in obese mice, which has demonstrated a pro-inflammatory role in the immune system in other studies [51,52]

Adiponectin is an adipokine that is typically reduced in cases of type 2 diabetes and obesity, and a prior murine model of diet-induced obesity involving a calvarial bone defect found that adiponectin promotes the migration of bone marrow-derived MSCs, as well as bone formation [62,63].
CRP is strongly associated with obesity in human studies [43,44].
At baseline, serum levels of leptin, resistin, and CRP were significantly higher in obese mice than in lean mice (Figure 5).
Rough Ti disks were fabricated by sand-blasting the pretreated smooth Ti with 0.25-0.50 mm coarse-grit corundum and acid etching in a HCl and H2SO4 solution.

Rough-hydrophilic Ti disks were produced following the same protocol to fabricate rough Ti disks but, to prevent air exposure, rinsed under a nitrogen atmosphere and sealed in an isotonic NaCl solution.

Scanning electron microscopy was used to qualitatively assess surface roughness (SEM, Zeiss Auriga, Carl Zeiss, Germany), and confocal microscopy was used for quantitative assessment (LSM 980 Laser Scanning Microscope, Germany) with a 20x objective and a total measurement area of 790 um x 790 um (n=3).
A Ram-Hart goniometer was used to measure the sessile drop contact angle to determine surface hydrophilicity (Model 100-25a, Rame-Hart Instrument Co., Succasunna, NJ).
Rough-hydrophilic Ti (theta = 0deg) was more hydrophilic, and rough Ti (theta = 93deg) was more hydrophobic, as determined by analysis of the water contact angle (Figure 1B).

The wettability of rough and rough-hydrophilic Ti surfaces was correlated with the quantity of carbon in the oxide layer. Rough Ti demonstrated a higher quantity of carbon in the oxide layer than rough-hydrophilic Ti.

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2022 - Canonical Wnt signaling enhances pro-inflammatory response to titanium by macrophages.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Biomaterial characteristics like surface roughness and wettability can determine the phenotype of macrophages following implantation. We have demonstrated that inhibiting Wnt ligand secretion abolishes macrophage polarization in vitro and in vivo ; however, the role of canonical Wnt signaling in macrophage activation in response to physical and chemical biomaterial cues is unknown. The aim of this study was to understand whether canonical Wnt signaling affects the response of macrophages to titanium (Ti) surface roughness or wettability in vitro and in vivo . Activating canonical Wnt signaling increased expression of toll-like receptors and interleukin receptors and secreted pro-inflammatory cytokines and reduced anti-inflammatory cytokines on Ti, regardless of surface properties. Inhibiting canonical Wnt signaling reduced pro-inflammatory cytokines on all Ti surfaces and increased  anti-inflammatory  cytokines  on  rough  or  rough-hydrophilic  Ti. In  vivo ,  activating  canonical  Wnt signaling  increased  total  macrophages,  pro-inflammatory  macrophages,  and  T  cells  and  decreased  antiinflammatory macrophages on both smooth and rough-hydrophilic implants. Functionally, canonical Wnt activation increases pro-inflammatory macrophage response to cell and cell-extracellular matrix lysates. These results demonstrate that activating canonical Wnt signaling primes macrophages to a pro-inflammatory phenotype that affects their response to Ti implants in vitro and in vivo .

edges.evidence_phrase: To  understand  the  significance  of  canonical  Wnt  signaling  on macrophage activation, nave macrophages were plated on tissue culture polystyrene (TCPS) and treated with either activators (recombinant mouse Wnt3a (5, 10, 15, 20, or 25 ng/mL, R & D Systems, Minneapolis, MN), AZD 2858 (0.1, 1, 2.5, or 5 u g/mL, Cayman Chemical, Ann Arbor, MI), or SB 216763 (8, 16, 24, 32 u g/mL, Cayman Chemical) or an inhibitor (PNU 74654, 1, 2, 3, or 4 u g/mL, Cayman Chemical) of canonical Wnt signaling. Small molecules were resuspended in dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) before use and DMSO was used as control. We selected Wnt3a because in our previous studies, Wnt3a was one of the earliest canonical Wnt ligands expressed by macrophages in response to Ti implants and also  had  a  strong  pro-inflammatory  effect  on  macrophages  [9].  AZD 2858 and SB 216763 are potent selective inhibitors of GSK3 that induce cytoplasmic accumulation of beta -catenin and beta -catenin-dependent gene expression [37 -40]. PNU 74654 disrupts canonical Wnt signaling by D. Avery et al.

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2022 - Canonical Wnt signaling enhances pro-inflammatory response to titanium by macrophages.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: To  understand  the  significance  of  canonical  Wnt  signaling  on macrophage  activation,  macrophages  were  cultured  with  activators (Wnt3a, AZD 2858, and SB 216763) or an inhibitor (PNU 74654) of canonical Wnt signaling. Activation of canonical Wnt signaling lead to upregulation of canonical Wnt co-receptors Lrp5, Lrp6 (Fig. 3A) , Wnt canonical modulator Axin2 and toll-like receptors Tlr2, Tlr4, Tlr5, Tlr7, and Tlr9 (Fig. 4). In contrast, inhibiting canonical Wnt signaling with PNU 74654 decreased expression of Il6ra , Tlr1 , Tlr2 , Tlr4 , Tlr5 , Tlr7 , Tlr9 ,  and  increased  expression  of Il4ra and Il10ra (Figs.  3A  and  4). Secretion of inflammatory cytokines IL-1 beta ,  IL-6,  and  TNFalpha in conditioned media increased when macrophages were treated with canonical Wnt signaling activators (Wnt3a, AZD 2858 or SB 216763) (Fig. 3B). Furthermore, activation of canonical Wnt signaling resulted in a dose dependent decrease of IL-4 and IL-10 (Fig. 3B).

edges.evidence_phrase: To  understand  the  significance  of  canonical  Wnt  signaling  on macrophage activation, nave macrophages were plated on tissue culture polystyrene (TCPS) and treated with either activators (recombinant mouse Wnt3a (5, 10, 15, 20, or 25 ng/mL, R & D Systems, Minneapolis, MN), AZD 2858 (0.1, 1, 2.5, or 5 u g/mL, Cayman Chemical, Ann Arbor, MI), or SB 216763 (8, 16, 24, 32 u g/mL, Cayman Chemical) or an inhibitor (PNU 74654, 1, 2, 3, or 4 u g/mL, Cayman Chemical) of canonical Wnt signaling. Small molecules were resuspended in dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) before use and DMSO was used as control. We selected Wnt3a because in our previous studies, Wnt3a was one of the earliest canonical Wnt ligands expressed by macrophages in response to Ti implants and also  had  a  strong  pro-inflammatory  effect  on  macrophages  [9].  AZD 2858 and SB 216763 are potent selective inhibitors of GSK3 that induce cytoplasmic accumulation of beta -catenin and beta -catenin-dependent gene expression [37 -40]. PNU 74654 disrupts canonical Wnt signaling by D. Avery et al.

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2022 - Canonical Wnt signaling enhances pro-inflammatory response to titanium by macrophages.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Macrophages altered expression canonical Wnt co-receptors ( Lrp5, Lrp6 ), interleukin receptors ( Il1r1, Il1rn, Il4ra, Il6ra, Il10ra ) and TLRs ( Tlr1,  Tlr2,  Tlr4,  Tlr5, and Tlr9 )  in  response  to  Ti  physicochemical characteristics. Smooth Ti surfaces upregulated the most Axin2 , Lrp5, Lrp6, Il1r1, Il6ra, Tlr1, Tlr2, Tlr4, Tlr5, and Tlr9 when compared to rough and rough-hydrophilic Ti surfaces (Fig. 6). In the other hand, roughhydrophilic  Ti  surfaces  upregulated  the  most Il1rn , Il4ra and Il10ra . Treatment with canonical Wnt signaling activators increased expression Axin2 , Lrp5, Lrp6, Il1r1, Il6ra, Tlr1, Tlr2, Tlr4, Tlr5, and Tlr9 in TCPS and all Ti surfaces. Furthermore, treatment with Wnt3a, AZD 2858 and SB 216763 decrease expression of Il1rn , Il4ra and Il10ra in all Ti surfaces when  compared  to  vehicle  treatment.  Inhibition  of  canonical  Wnt signaling with PNU 74654 decreased expression of Axin2, Lrp5, Lrp6, Il1r1, Il6ra, Tlr1, Tlr2, Tlr4, Tlr5, and Tlr9 in smooth and rough Ti surfaces  when  compared to  vehicle  group.  Additionally,  treatment  with PNU 74654 increased expression of Il1rn, Il4ra and Il10ra in TCPS and all Ti surfaces. Together these data suggests that activation of the canonical Wnt  pathway  increases  secretion  of  pro-inflammatory  cytokines  and interleukin receptor and TLR expression.
edges.source: Biomaterial Surface Properties and Immune Modulation:smooth titanium surface
edges.target: Biomaterial Surface Properties and Immune Modulation:rough-hydrophilic titanium surface

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2022 - Canonical Wnt signaling enhances pro-inflammatory response to titanium by macrophages.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Higher surface roughness and increased topography was evident in SEM images on both rough and rough-hydrophilic Ti as result of the sand-blasting  and  acid  etching  as  compared  to  smooth  Ti  (Fig.  1A). Quantitatively, rough (Sa = 3.26 u m) and rough-hydrophilic (Sa = 3.24 u m) were significantly rougher than smooth Ti (Sa = 0.74 u m). We did not  identify  differences  in  surface  roughness  between  rough  Ti  and rough-hydrophilic  Ti  samples  either  qualitatively  or  quantitatively. Water contact angle measurements (Fig. 1B) showed that the most hydrophobic  surface  was  rough  Ti  ( theta = 110.5  )  while  the  roughhydrophilic Ti ( theta = 0  ) was superhydrophilic. Smooth Ti was normal ( theta = 63  ).  Wettability  of  our  Ti  samples  correlated  with  the  carbon content  of  the  oxide  layer.  Surface  chemical  composition  (Fig.  1C) showed that rough Ti, the most hydrophobic material tested, had the most C content in the oxide layer, while rough-hydrophilic Ti had the lowest C content in the oxide layer.
edges.source: Biomaterial Surface Properties and Immune Modulation:rough titanium surface
edges.target: Biomaterial Surface Properties and Immune Modulation:surface hydrophilicity

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2022 - Canonical Wnt signaling enhances pro-inflammatory response to titanium by macrophages.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: When canonical Wnt signaling was activated with AZD 2858 or SB 216763, macrophages were elevated locally around smooth and roughhydrophilic Ti implants on day 3 post-implantation when compared to vehicle control or PNU 74654 (Fig. 9). Moreover, treatment with AZD 2858  or  SB  216763  increased  pro-inflammatory  macrophages  and reduced anti-inflammatory  macrophages  on  both  smooth  and  roughhydrophilic Ti implants when compared to vehicle or PNU 74654. Inhibition of canonical Wnt signaling with PNU 74654 slightly increased pro-inflammatory macrophages and reduced anti-inflammatory macrophages on rough-hydrophilic Ti implants when compared to the vehicle group. Treatment with PNU 74654 reduced T cells on both smooth and rough-hydrophilic Ti implants (Fig. 9). At day 7 post-implantation, all macrophages (total, pro- and anti-inflammatory) and T cells were lower in mice with rough-hydrophilic Ti implants when compared to smooth Ti implants. Activation of canonical Wnt signaling increased levels of D. Avery et al.
edges.source: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:pro-inflammatory macrophages
edges.target: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:anti-inflammatory macrophages

Moreover, treatment with AZD 2858  or  SB  216763  increased  pro-inflammatory  macrophages  and reduced anti-inflammatory  macrophages  on  both  smooth  and  roughhydrophilic Ti implants when compared to vehicle or PNU 74654. Inhibition of canonical Wnt signaling with PNU 74654 slightly increased pro-inflammatory macrophages and reduced anti-inflammatory macrophages on rough-hydrophilic Ti implants when compared to the vehicle group. Treatment with PNU 74654 reduced T cells on both smooth and rough-hydrophilic Ti implants (Fig. 9). At day 7 post-implantation, all macrophages (total, pro- and anti-inflammatory) and T cells were lower in mice with rough-hydrophilic Ti implants when compared to smooth Ti implants. Activation of canonical Wnt signaling increased levels of D. Avery et al.
edges.source: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:T cells
edges.target: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:pro-inflammatory macrophages

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2022 - Canonical Wnt signaling enhances pro-inflammatory response to titanium by macrophages.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: blood and bone marrow after treatment with AZD 2858 and SB 216763 but CD8 + T cells increased only in blood. Inhibition of canonical Wnt signaling by PNU 74654 treatment did not have an effect on macrophages or T cells, with proportions similar to vehicle-injected controls
edges.source: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:CD4+ T cells
edges.target: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:CD8+ T cells

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2022 - Canonical Wnt signaling enhances pro-inflammatory response to titanium by macrophages.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: blood and bone marrow after treatment with AZD 2858 and SB 216763 but CD8 + T cells increased only in blood. Inhibition of canonical Wnt signaling by PNU 74654 treatment did not have an effect on macrophages or T cells, with proportions similar to vehicle-injected controls

edges.target: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:CD4+ T cells

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2022 - Canonical Wnt signaling enhances pro-inflammatory response to titanium by macrophages.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Cytokine levels in the peri-implant environment were affected by the surface modifications on Ti implants and by treatments with Wnt canonical activators and inhibitors. Peri-implant levels of IL-1 beta , IL-6, IL12, IL-23, CCL2, and TNFalpha were reduced in rough-hydrophilic implants when compared to smooth implants at 3 and 7 days post-implantation (Figs.  9  and  10).  IL-10  and  TGFbeta 1  levels  were  higher  in  roughhydrophilic  implants  when  compared  to  smooth  implants  at  3  days post-implantation (Fig. 9)  and were lower in rough-hydrophilic than smooth  implants  at  day  7  (Fig.  10).  Activation  of  Wnt  canonical signaling by AZD 2858 or SB 216763 increased peri-implant levels of all pro-inflammatory  cytokines  measured  in  both  implants  smooth  and rough-hydrophilic and both day 3 and day 7 (Figs. 9 and 10). In contrast, treatment  with  PNU  74654  decreased  levels  of  TGFbeta 1  on  roughhydrophilic implants at day 3 when compared to vehicle control animals, and IL-6, CCL2, TNFalpha , and TGFbeta 1 on smooth Ti implants at day 7 when compared to vehicle control.
edges.source: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:IL-1beta
edges.target: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:IL-6

Peri-implant levels of IL-1 beta , IL-6, IL12, IL-23, CCL2, and TNFalpha were reduced in rough-hydrophilic implants when compared to smooth implants at 3 and 7 days post-implantation (Figs.  9  and  10).  IL-10  and  TGFbeta 1  levels  were  higher  in  roughhydrophilic  implants  when  compared  to  smooth  implants  at  3  days post-implantation (Fig. 9)  and were lower in rough-hydrophilic than smooth  implants  at  day  7  (Fig.  10).  Activation  of  Wnt  canonical signaling by AZD 2858 or SB 216763 increased peri-implant levels of all pro-inflammatory  cytokines  measured  in  both  implants  smooth  and rough-hydrophilic and both day 3 and day 7 (Figs. 9 and 10). In contrast, treatment  with  PNU  74654  decreased  levels  of  TGFbeta 1  on  roughhydrophilic implants at day 3 when compared to vehicle control animals, and IL-6, CCL2, TNFalpha , and TGFbeta 1 on smooth Ti implants at day 7 when compared to vehicle control.
edges.source: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:TNFalpha
edges.target: In Vivo Immune Response to Implants:CCL2

Activation  of  Wnt  canonical signaling by AZD 2858 or SB 216763 increased peri-implant levels of all pro-inflammatory  cytokines  measured  in  both  implants  smooth  and rough-hydrophilic and both day 3 and day 7 (Figs. 9 and 10). In contrast, treatment  with  PNU  74654  decreased  levels  of  TGFbeta 1  on  roughhydrophilic implants at day 3 when compared to vehicle control animals, and IL-6, CCL2, TNFalpha , and TGFbeta 1 on smooth Ti implants at day 7 when compared to vehicle control.

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2023 - Contribution of αβ T cells to macrophage polarization and MSC recruitment and proliferation on titan.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Physiochemical cues like topography and wettability can impact the inflammatory response and tissue integration after biomaterial implantation. T cells are essential for immunomodulation of innate immune cells and play an important role in the host response to biomaterial implantation. This study aimed to understand how CD4 + and CD8 + T cell subsets, members of the alphabeta T cell family, polarize in response to smooth, rough, or rough-hydrophilic titanium (Ti) implants and whether their presence modulates immune cell crosstalk and mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) recruitment following biomaterial implantation. Post-implantation in mice, we found that CD4 + and CD8 + T cell subsets polarized differentially in response to modified Ti surfaces. Additionally, mice lacking alphabeta T cells had significantly more proinflammatory macrophages, fewer anti-inflammatory macrophages, and reduced MSC recruitment in response to modified Ti post-implantation than alphabeta T cell -competent mice. Our results demonstrate that T cell activation plays a significant role during the inflammatory response to implanted biomaterials, contributing to macrophage polarization and MSC recruitment and proliferation, and the absence of alphabeta T cells compromises new bone formation at the implantation site.
edges.source: Immune Cell Response to Biomaterials:alphabeta T cells

This study aimed to understand how CD4 + and CD8 + T cell subsets, members of the alphabeta T cell family, polarize in response to smooth, rough, or rough-hydrophilic titanium (Ti) implants and whether their presence modulates immune cell crosstalk and mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) recruitment following biomaterial implantation. Post-implantation in mice, we found that CD4 + and CD8 + T cell subsets polarized differentially in response to modified Ti surfaces. Additionally, mice lacking alphabeta T cells had significantly more proinflammatory macrophages, fewer anti-inflammatory macrophages, and reduced MSC recruitment in response to modified Ti post-implantation than alphabeta T cell -competent mice. Our results demonstrate that T cell activation plays a significant role during the inflammatory response to implanted biomaterials, contributing to macrophage polarization and MSC recruitment and proliferation, and the absence of alphabeta T cells compromises new bone formation at the implantation site.
edges.source: Immune Cell Response to Biomaterials:alphabeta T cells
edges.target: Mesenchymal Stem Cell (MSC) Recruitment and Proliferation:MSC recruitment

edges.evidence_phrase: We explored the response of innate immune cells to physiochemical cues within the bone marrow. At 3 days postimplantation, total and pro-inflammatory macrophages did not show differences in response to modified Ti implants ( Fig. 2 ). Antiinflammatory macrophages were higher on rough and rough hydrophilic Ti implants, and neutrophils and dendritic cells were lower on rough hydrophilic Ti. At 7 days post-implantation, total macrophages, pro and anti-inflammatory macrophages, neutrophils, and dendritic cells were lower on rough and rough hydrophilic implants. These innate immune cells were the lowest in response to rough hydrophilic Ti implants at day 14 postimplantation.
edges.target: Immune Cell Response to Biomaterials:dendritic cells

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2023 - Contribution of αβ T cells to macrophage polarization and MSC recruitment and proliferation on titan.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: To investigate the role of helper T cell subsets in the healing response post-biomaterial implantation, we immunophenotyped CD4 + T cell subsets from bone marrow in the peri-implant mi- croenvironment. At postoperative day 3, Th2, Th22, and CD4 + Treg populations were significantly higher in response to rough hydrophilic Ti implants, while Th1, Th2, and Th17 subsets were lower ( Fig. 4 ). On day 7, Th1, Th2, Th9, Th17, and CD4 + Treg subsets were significantly lower in response to rough hydrophilic Ti. Th22 populations were lower in response to rough Ti implants on day 7. On day 14 post-implantation, Th1, Th2, Th9, and Th17 populations were the lowest in response to rough hydrophilic Ti. Th22 and CD4 + Treg populations were lower in response to rough and rough hydrophilic Ti on day 14.

At postoperative day 3, Th2, Th22, and CD4 + Treg populations were significantly higher in response to rough hydrophilic Ti implants, while Th1, Th2, and Th17 subsets were lower ( Fig. 4 ). On day 7, Th1, Th2, Th9, Th17, and CD4 + Treg subsets were significantly lower in response to rough hydrophilic Ti. Th22 populations were lower in response to rough Ti implants on day 7. On day 14 post-implantation, Th1, Th2, Th9, and Th17 populations were the lowest in response to rough hydrophilic Ti. Th22 and CD4 + Treg populations were lower in response to rough and rough hydrophilic Ti on day 14.

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2023 - Contribution of αβ T cells to macrophage polarization and MSC recruitment and proliferation on titan.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Physiochemical cues like topography and wettability can impact the inflammatory response and tissue integration after biomaterial implantation. T cells are essential for immunomodulation of innate immune cells and play an important role in the host response to biomaterial implantation. This study aimed to understand how CD4 + and CD8 + T cell subsets, members of the alphabeta T cell family, polarize in response to smooth, rough, or rough-hydrophilic titanium (Ti) implants and whether their presence modulates immune cell crosstalk and mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) recruitment following biomaterial implantation. Post-implantation in mice, we found that CD4 + and CD8 + T cell subsets polarized differentially in response to modified Ti surfaces. Additionally, mice lacking alphabeta T cells had significantly more proinflammatory macrophages, fewer anti-inflammatory macrophages, and reduced MSC recruitment in response to modified Ti post-implantation than alphabeta T cell -competent mice. Our results demonstrate that T cell activation plays a significant role during the inflammatory response to implanted biomaterials, contributing to macrophage polarization and MSC recruitment and proliferation, and the absence of alphabeta T cells compromises new bone formation at the implantation site.
edges.target: Immune Cell Response to Biomaterials:alphabeta T cells

Post-implantation in mice, we found that CD4 + and CD8 + T cell subsets polarized differentially in response to modified Ti surfaces. Additionally, mice lacking alphabeta T cells had significantly more proinflammatory macrophages, fewer anti-inflammatory macrophages, and reduced MSC recruitment in response to modified Ti post-implantation than alphabeta T cell -competent mice. Our results demonstrate that T cell activation plays a significant role during the inflammatory response to implanted biomaterials, contributing to macrophage polarization and MSC recruitment and proliferation, and the absence of alphabeta T cells compromises new bone formation at the implantation site.

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2023 - Contribution of αβ T cells to macrophage polarization and MSC recruitment and proliferation on titan.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: However, their role in tissue healing and regeneration is less clear. Recently, T cells have been shown to play an essential role in soft tissue healing and repair, including fibrosis [ 15 , 18 , 34 , 35 , 51 , 62 , 63 ]. While secondary lymphoid organs, such as lymph nodes, are the primary sites of initiation of T cells, the tissue where the T cells reside alters the antigen receptor repertoire compared with those in the systemic immune system, resulting in tissue-specific T cell responses [ 18 , 19 , 27 , 51 , 64 , 65 ]. Studies have shown the paradoxical effects of T cells during tissue healing and regeneration. T cell depletion increased mortality in a model of liver regeneration, but a lack of T cells led to scarless skin regeneration [ 66 , 67 ]. In biomaterial implantation, T cells are usually associated with chronic inflammatory responses and fibrotic tissue formation in a process known as foreign body response [ 16 , 17 , 68 ]. However, recent studies have shown that T cells, specifically some CD4 + subsets, are positively associated with the pro-regenerative or healing response [63] . Our results emphasize the significance of alphabeta T cells in the inflammatory response to biomaterials showing that alphabeta T cells are essential to modulate innate immune cell response and resolve the inflammatory response. Specifically, in Tcra -/ -mice, we observed a significant increase of pro-inflammatory macrophages and a reduction of anti-inflammatory macrophages in all time points measured compared to alphabeta -competent mice. Conventional T cells, specifically Th2 and Tregs, are essential for suppressing inflammatory innate immune cells and promoting anti-inflammatory macrophage polarization by producing anti-inflammatory cytokines such as IL-4, IL-10, and IL-13 [ 5 , 51 , 69 ]. In vitro and in vivo studies have shown the effect of Th2 and Treg in producing a more anti-inflammatory microenvironment and skewing macrophage activation towards an anti-inflammatory phenotype [ 5 , 38 ]. In vivo studies have also shown the immunomodulatory effect of Treg adoptive transfer in graft and solid organ transplantation [70-73] . Tregs suppress CD4 + and CD8 + T cells directly and indirectly and antigen-presenting cell proliferation and function [74] . Tregs possess different contactdependent mechanisms, such as the expression of perforin and granzymes, which directly disrupt the target cell membrane resulting in apoptosis and expressing inhibitory molecules (CTLA-4, PD-1) which suppress dendritic cells, macrophages, and other T cells [ 75 , 76 ]. Tregs also secrete immunomodulatory cytokines such as IL-10, IL-35, and TGFbeta 1 that suppress alphabeta T cells and polarize macrophages towards an anti-inflammatory phenotype [77-79] . Altogether, we show that a lack of alphabeta T cells skews macrophages into a pro-inflammatory polarization and inhibits suppression of the inflammatory response.

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2023 - Contribution of αβ T cells to macrophage polarization and MSC recruitment and proliferation on titan.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: alphabeta T cells regulate their presence at the implantation site by physical contact or secretion of important inflammatory cytokines. Th1 cells are essential in secreting IFNgamma and TNFalpha , inducing other immune cell activation, and promoting macrophage nitric oxide production [ 94 , 95 ]. Here, we show that Th1 polarization corresponds with higher pro-inflammatory macrophages in response to smooth Ti. Furthermore, we also identified Th17 cells in the periimplant tissue of smooth Ti implants. Th1 and Th17 have previously shown a similar response to clinically used orthopedic implants, polarizing significantly more in response to polyetheretherketone (PEEK) and stainless steel than Ti implants [61] . This Th1/Th17 effect has been observed previously where exacerbated levels of IL-6, IL-8, IL-17, IFNgamma , and TNFalpha have been found in the fibrotic tissue surrounding silicone implants [62] . Th17 cells secrete pro-inflammatory IL-17 that enhances the pro-inflammatory microenvironment by signaling neutrophils and macrophages to upregulate inflammatory factors. Results from our study confirm that Th17 cells recruit and support more neutrophils since Th17 cells were elevated in smooth and rough Ti implants, which were also the implants with elevated neutrophil presence over time. Moreover, Th17 can also induce bone destruction by stimulating osteoclastogenesis and secretion of TNFalpha and IL-6 [ 96 , 97 ].

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2024 - CD4+ and CD8+ T cells reduce inflammation and promote bone healing in response to titanium implants.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: T cells are adaptive immune cells essential in pathogenic response, cancer, and autoimmune disorders. During the integration of biomaterials with host tissue, T cells modify the local inflammatory environment by releasing cytokines that promote inflammatory resolution following implantation. T cells are vital for the modulation of innate immune cells, recruitment and proliferation of mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs), and formation of functional tissue around the biomaterial implant. We have demonstrated that deficiency of alphabeta T cells promotes macrophage polarization towards a pro-inflammatory phenotype and attenuates MSC recruitment and proliferation in vitro and in vivo. The goal of this study was to understand how CD4 + and CD8 + T cells, subsets of the alphabeta T cell family, impact the inflammatory response to titanium (Ti) biomaterials. Deficiency of either CD4 + or CD8 + T cells increased the proportion of proinflammatory macrophages, lowered anti-inflammatory macrophages, and diminished MSC recruitment in vitro and in vivo . In addition, new bone formation at the implantation site was significantly reduced in T cell-deficient mice compared to T cell-competent mice. Deficiency of CD4 + T cells exacerbated these effects com pared to CD8 + T cell deficiency. Our results show the importance of CD4 + and CD8 + T cells in modulating the inflammatory response and promoting new bone formation in response to modified Ti implants.
edges.source: Immune Cell Response to Biomaterials:CD4+ T cells
edges.target: Immune Cell Response to Biomaterials:CD8+ T cells

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2024 - CD4+ and CD8+ T cells reduce inflammation and promote bone healing in response to titanium implants.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: We identified production of pro-inflammatory (IL-2, IL-6, IL9, IL-17A, IL-17F, TNFalpha , IFNgamma ) and anti-inflammatory (IL-4, IL5, IL-10, IL-13, IL-22) cytokines in peri-implant tissue. At postoperative day 3, levels of pro-inflammatory IL-9 and IL-17A and anti-inflammatory IL-4, IL-5, and IL-13 were significantly lower in Cd4 -/ -mice ( Fig. 7 ). IL-2 and TNFalpha production was higher in Cd4 -/ -mice when compared to WT mice, specifically in response to smooth and rough Ti implants. IL-22 production was the highest in WT mice but did not show significant differences between Cd4 -/ -and Cd8 -/ -mice. Production of IL-6, IL-10, IL-17F, and IFNgamma did not demonstrate significant differences between Cd4 -/ -, Cd8 -/ -, and WT mice.

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2024 - CD4+ and CD8+ T cells reduce inflammation and promote bone healing in response to titanium implants.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: On day 14 post-implantation, circulating levels of proinflammatory IL-6 and IFNgamma , as well as anti-inflammatory IL-4 and IL-10, were higher in Cd4 -/ -and Cd8 -/ -mice compared to WT mice. Production of IL-4 was the lowest in response to rough hydrophilic Ti implants in both WT and Cd8 -/ -mice, although IL-4 levels were the highest in response to these substrates in Cd4 -/ -mice. IL-5 levels were significantly lower in Cd4 -/ -mice. Production of other inflammatory cytokines did not show significant differences between Cd4 -/ -, Cd8 -/ -, and WT mice.

edges.source_paper: Avery et al. - 2024 - CD4+ and CD8+ T cells reduce inflammation and promote bone healing in response to titanium implants.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: We analyzed bone-to-implant contact and identified that WT mice produced significantly more bone around all modified Ti implants than Cd4 -/ -and Cd8 -/ -mice ( Fig. 8 A and 8 B). Cd4 -/ -mice showed the lowest peri-implant bone in response to smooth Ti implants, while the WT mice receiving rough-hydrophilic Ti implants demonstrated the highest bone-implant contact. We also quanti- fied new bone formation by analyzing bone volume as a proportion of total volume, and similarly, WT mice showed more new bone formation in response to all modified Ti implants when compared to Cd4 -/ -and Cd8 -/ -mice (Supplemental Fig. 1). Cd4 -/ -and Cd8 -/ -mice receiving rough or rough hydrophilic Ti implants produced more new bone than those receiving smooth Ti implants. Cd4 -/ -mice receiving rough hydrophilic implants showed lower bone formation than Cd8 -/ -mice and WT mice.
edges.target: Bone Formation and Osseointegration:New bone volume

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: The immune system plays an integral role in coordinating the inflammatory response and healing outcomes. My lab has demonstrated that macrophages orchestrate the interplay between innate immunity, adaptive immunity, and mesenchymal stem cell recruitment [1]. However, as we age, cells  lose  function  and  become  senescent,  including  macrophages.  Understanding  age-related immune  dysfunction  represents  an  essential  step  toward  improving  surgical  outcomes  in  our rapidly aging population, particularly as poor healing outcomes  after implant surgeries disproportionately affect older patients.
edges.source: Aging and Immune Dysfunction:age-related immune dysfunction

Understanding  age-related immune  dysfunction  represents  an  essential  step  toward  improving  surgical  outcomes  in  our rapidly aging population, particularly as poor healing outcomes  after implant surgeries disproportionately affect older patients.

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Intellectual Merit: The normal inflammatory response progresses through an initial inflammatory stage that clears debris and  pathogens,  followed  by  an  anti-inflammatory  stage that is immunomodulatory  and  pro-healing.  The  timing  between  these  stages  is  critical  for  tissue regeneration [1]. Recent work from our lab demonstrated that after titanium implant placement in older  animals,  we  observe  prolonged  inflammation,  increased  pro-inflammatory  cytokine production,  and  lower-quality  healing.  In  young  mice,  expression  of  inflammatory  cytokines elevated immediately post-surgery but returned to baseline by 21 days. In aged mice, however, expression remained elevated past 21 days, resulting in chronic inflammation and poor healing. Macrophages were among the most impacted cell types, which is significant given their central role in mediating pro- and anti-inflammatory responses.
edges.source: Aging and Immune Dysfunction:prolonged inflammation in aged mice
edges.target: Aging and Immune Dysfunction:chronic inflammation in aged mice

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: My research objective is to investigate biomaterial-induced senescence in immune cells and use cellular rejuvenation methods to eliminate the senescent phenotype. I hypothesize that rejuvenation of senescent macrophages will accelerate resolution of the pro-inflammatory phase, reduce chronic inflammation, and improve wound healing in aged populations. While building on my advisor's expertise in macrophage biology [1,9], my project uniquely contributes by studying a novel therapeutic approach, as no prior studies have attempted to rejuvenate immune cells for applications in aging-related healing deficits.

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: My research objective is to investigate biomaterial-induced senescence in immune cells and use cellular rejuvenation methods to eliminate the senescent phenotype. While building on my advisor's expertise in macrophage biology [1,9], my project uniquely contributes by studying a novel therapeutic approach, as no prior studies have attempted to rejuvenate immune cells for applications in aging-related healing deficits.

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Refunctionalization will be assessed by: (1) senescence marker reduction (p16INK4A, senescenceassociated beta-galactosidase expression measured by flow cytometry and immunofluorescence, with success defined as >=50% reduction), (2) macrophage identity main tenance (CD45+/CD11b+/MHCII+/F4/80+/Mac+ by flow cytometry [1]), and (3) functional restoration (phagocytosis  assays).  Inflammatory  potential  will  be  measured  through  cytokine  secretion profiles by ELISA for TNFalpha, IL -1beta, IL -6, IL-10, TGFbeta.
edges.source: Macrophage Biology and Rejuvenation:macrophage identity markers

edges.source: Macrophage Biology and Rejuvenation:macrophage identity markers

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Immune cell populations will be quantified by flow cytometry using markers for total macrophages (CD45+/CD11b+/MHCII+/Cd11c-/F4/80+), M1 macrophages (CD206-/CD80+), and M2 macrophages (CD80-/CD206+). Serum cytokines (TNFalpha,  IL -1beta,  IL -6,  IL-10,  TGFbeta)  will  be measured by ELISA. Bone formation will be assessed by micro-CT for Bone/Tissue volume and bone mineral density. I expect refunctionalized macrophage transfer to reduce pro-inflammatory cytokines,  accelerate  M2  polarization,  and  result  in  significantly  increased  BV/TV  at  day  28 compared to aged controls.

Inflammatory  potential  will  be  measured  through  cytokine  secretion profiles by ELISA for TNFalpha, IL -1beta, IL -6, IL-10, TGFbeta.
edges.target: Macrophage Biology and Rejuvenation:macrophage identity markers

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Year 2 Aim 2 - Evaluate therapeutic efficacy of refunctionalized macrophages in aged mice with  titanium  implants: My  lab  previously  demonstrated  that  adoptive  transfer  of  antiinflammatory  macrophages  reduces  pro-inflammatory  responses  and  results  in  improved  bone regeneration [9]. I will use these techniques to evaluate whether refunctionalized macrophages resolve chronic inflammation and improve healing at implant sites. MaFIA mice (old, middle-aged, and young) will be injected with AP20187 for 3 days pre-implantation to deplete endogenous macrophages to <10% baseline. Mice will receive titanium implant surgery followed by immediate macrophage adoptive transfer through tail-vein injection. Experimental groups will repeat for all mouse age groups as: (1) transfer of aged untreated macrophages, (2) transfer of refunctionalized macrophages, and (3) transfer of young untreated macrophages. Mice will be sacrificed at 7, 14, and 28 days post-implantation.
edges.source: Macrophage Biology and Rejuvenation:adoptive transfer of anti-inflammatory macrophages
edges.target: Implant Healing and Bone Regeneration:bone regeneration

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Year 1 Aim 1 -Compare chemical reprogramming versus OSKM-mediated refunctionalization  of  macrophages  across  age  groups: Chemical  reprogramming  offers significant advantages over genetic methods for cellular rejuvenation. Chemical cocktails achieve more uniform distribution in culture media compared to plasmid transfection, which suffers from variable  uptake  efficiency  [2].  The  VC6TF  cocktail  (valproic  acid,  CHIR-99021,  E-616452, tranylcypromine,  and  forskolin)  achieves  rejuvenation  through  direct  epigenetic  modulation, including  demethylation  and  histone  acetylation  changes  [3,5],  and  demonstrates  rejuvenation within 4 days compared to 10-14 days for OSKM reprogramming [3,4]. I hypothesize that the VC6TF  chemical  cocktail  will  outperform  OSKM  plasmid  transfection  at  macrophage refunctionalization because the chemicals specifically demethylate chromatin  marks associated  with  senescence  [5]  and  distribute  evenly  throughout  culture  media,  unlike plasmid transfection which has inherently low and variable efficiency in primary immune cells [2]. Additionally, individual components of the VC6TF cocktail exhibit anti-inflammatory properties.  Valproic  acid  downregulates  pro-inflammatory  cytokines  (TNFalpha,  IL -1beta,  IL -6)  in macrophages  [6],  while  TGFbeta  pathway  modulation  by  E -616452  can  reduce  inflammatory responses [7]. If chemical refunctionalization cannot be achieved, chemical treatment should still  reduce  inflammatory  markers  in  aged  macrophages,  providing  therapeutic  benefit through adoptive transfer of cells with reduced inflammatory potential.
edges.source: Cellular Reprogramming and Epigenetic Modulation:chemical reprogramming
edges.target: Cellular Reprogramming and Epigenetic Modulation:VC6TF chemical cocktail

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Macrophages will be isolated from bone marrow of mice in three age groups: young (3 months), middle-aged (10 months), and old (20 months). Experimental groups at each age will include: (1) untreated controls, (2) OSKM plasmid transfection with doxycycline induction, and (3) VC6TF chemical cocktail added to media. All cells will be cultured for 4, 7, and 10 days. For chemical treatments, cells will receive VC6TF at established concentrations [3]: valproic acid (0.5 mM), CHIR99021 (10 uM), E -616452 (10 uM), tranylcypromine (10 uM), and forskolin (10 uM). For OSKM  treatment,  cells  will  be  transfected  with  plasmids  containing  OSKM  and  rtTA  with doxycycline (2 mcg/mL) added to induce expression.
edges.source: Cellular Reprogramming and Epigenetic Modulation:OSKM plasmid transfection
edges.target: Cellular Reprogramming and Epigenetic Modulation:valproic acid

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Year 1 Aim 1 -Compare chemical reprogramming versus OSKM-mediated refunctionalization  of  macrophages  across  age  groups: Chemical  reprogramming  offers significant advantages over genetic methods for cellular rejuvenation. Chemical cocktails achieve more uniform distribution in culture media compared to plasmid transfection, which suffers from variable  uptake  efficiency  [2].  The  VC6TF  cocktail  (valproic  acid,  CHIR-99021,  E-616452, tranylcypromine,  and  forskolin)  achieves  rejuvenation  through  direct  epigenetic  modulation, including  demethylation  and  histone  acetylation  changes  [3,5],  and  demonstrates  rejuvenation within 4 days compared to 10-14 days for OSKM reprogramming [3,4]. I hypothesize that the VC6TF  chemical  cocktail  will  outperform  OSKM  plasmid  transfection  at  macrophage refunctionalization because the chemicals specifically demethylate chromatin  marks associated  with  senescence  [5]  and  distribute  evenly  throughout  culture  media,  unlike plasmid transfection which has inherently low and variable efficiency in primary immune cells [2]. Additionally, individual components of the VC6TF cocktail exhibit anti-inflammatory properties.  Valproic  acid  downregulates  pro-inflammatory  cytokines  (TNFalpha,  IL -1beta,  IL -6)  in macrophages  [6],  while  TGFbeta  pathway  modulation  by  E -616452  can  reduce  inflammatory responses [7]. If chemical refunctionalization cannot be achieved, chemical treatment should still  reduce  inflammatory  markers  in  aged  macrophages,  providing  therapeutic  benefit through adoptive transfer of cells with reduced inflammatory potential.
edges.source: Cellular Reprogramming and Epigenetic Modulation:epigenetic modulation
edges.target: Cellular Reprogramming and Epigenetic Modulation:chemical reprogramming

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Intellectual Merit: The normal inflammatory response progresses through an initial inflammatory stage that clears debris and  pathogens,  followed  by  an  anti-inflammatory  stage that is immunomodulatory  and  pro-healing.  The  timing  between  these  stages  is  critical  for  tissue regeneration [1]. Recent work from our lab demonstrated that after titanium implant placement in older  animals,  we  observe  prolonged  inflammation,  increased  pro-inflammatory  cytokine production,  and  lower-quality  healing.  In  young  mice,  expression  of  inflammatory  cytokines elevated immediately post-surgery but returned to baseline by 21 days. In aged mice, however, expression remained elevated past 21 days, resulting in chronic inflammation and poor healing. Macrophages were among the most impacted cell types, which is significant given their central role in mediating pro- and anti-inflammatory responses.
edges.source: Implant Healing and Bone Regeneration:titanium implant
edges.target: Aging and Immune Dysfunction:prolonged inflammation in aged mice

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Year 2 Aim 2 - Evaluate therapeutic efficacy of refunctionalized macrophages in aged mice with  titanium  implants: My  lab  previously  demonstrated  that  adoptive  transfer  of  antiinflammatory  macrophages  reduces  pro-inflammatory  responses  and  results  in  improved  bone regeneration [9]. I will use these techniques to evaluate whether refunctionalized macrophages resolve chronic inflammation and improve healing at implant sites. MaFIA mice (old, middle-aged, and young) will be injected with AP20187 for 3 days pre-implantation to deplete endogenous macrophages to <10% baseline. Mice will receive titanium implant surgery followed by immediate macrophage adoptive transfer through tail-vein injection. Experimental groups will repeat for all mouse age groups as: (1) transfer of aged untreated macrophages, (2) transfer of refunctionalized macrophages, and (3) transfer of young untreated macrophages. Mice will be sacrificed at 7, 14, and 28 days post-implantation.
edges.source: Implant Healing and Bone Regeneration:bone regeneration
edges.target: Macrophage Biology and Rejuvenation:adoptive transfer of anti-inflammatory macrophages

I will use these techniques to evaluate whether refunctionalized macrophages resolve chronic inflammation and improve healing at implant sites. MaFIA mice (old, middle-aged, and young) will be injected with AP20187 for 3 days pre-implantation to deplete endogenous macrophages to <10% baseline. Mice will receive titanium implant surgery followed by immediate macrophage adoptive transfer through tail-vein injection. Experimental groups will repeat for all mouse age groups as: (1) transfer of aged untreated macrophages, (2) transfer of refunctionalized macrophages, and (3) transfer of young untreated macrophages. Mice will be sacrificed at 7, 14, and 28 days post-implantation.

edges.source_paper: Sheakley - 2025 - NSF GRFP Grant.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Immune cell populations will be quantified by flow cytometry using markers for total macrophages (CD45+/CD11b+/MHCII+/Cd11c-/F4/80+), M1 macrophages (CD206-/CD80+), and M2 macrophages (CD80-/CD206+). Serum cytokines (TNFalpha,  IL -1beta,  IL -6,  IL-10,  TGFbeta)  will  be measured by ELISA. Bone formation will be assessed by micro-CT for Bone/Tissue volume and bone mineral density. I expect refunctionalized macrophage transfer to reduce pro-inflammatory cytokines,  accelerate  M2  polarization,  and  result  in  significantly  increased  BV/TV  at  day  28 compared to aged controls.
edges.source: Implant Healing and Bone Regeneration:bone volume/total volume (BV/TV)

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Obesity is a metabolic disease that is the result of excessive body fat accumulation, negatively impacting health by increasing the risk of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and osteoarthritis [1,2]. Obesity is a global epidemic with an estimated prevalence of 40% among the world population, and it has been linked to the development of chronic inflammation [2]. Likewise, pro-inflammatory cytokine production is increased in animal models of high-fat diet (HFD)-induced obesity [3,4]. Chronic inflammation due to obesity plays a vital role in altering the function of adipose tissue, contributing to systemic insulin resistance and increasing immune cell infiltration of adipose tissue [1,5,6]. Neutrophils, macrophages, and T cells are among the immune cells previously shown to infiltrate the adipose tissue and promote systemic inflammation [6-10]. Macrophages within the adipose tissue in HFD-induced obesity models have been suggested to be the primary source of inflammatory factors in obesity, demonstrating increased polarization towards a pro-inflammatory phenotype [11-13]. Diet-induced obesity impairs T cell response in mice, with higher numbers of Th17 cells and reduced Treg cells in lymph nodes and spleen compared to a standard, normal-fat diet [14-16].

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Obesity is a metabolic disease resulting from excess body fat accumulation associated with chronic systemic inflammation. Obesity has been shown to impact the function and activity of neutrophils, macrophages, and T cells, contributing to higher circulating levels of proinflammatory cytokines. Biomaterial surface properties such as roughness and hydrophilicity can influence the behavior of immune cells in the peri-implant microenvironment. This study aimed to determine how obesity induced by a high-fat diet (HFD) affects the inflammatory response to modified titanium (Ti) implants and subsequent bone formation. Obese mice had significantly more neutrophils, pro-inflammatory macrophages, and T cells and fewer anti-inflammatory macrophages and mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) in the peri-implant tissue than lean mice. Obesity also increased circulating adipokines and pro-inflammatory cytokines when compared to lean animals. Bone formation around Ti implants was reduced in obese mice compared to controls. Adoptive transfer of bone marrow cells isolated from obese mice into wild-type mice demonstrated the localized impact of obesity on immune cell function and phenotype, promoting a pro-inflammatory peri-implant microenvironment and attenuating bone formation post-implantation. These results show that obesity significantly affects the inflammatory response to modified Ti implants, prolonging the pro-inflammatory response to the implanted biomaterial and compromising bone formation.

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Obesity is a metabolic disease that is the result of excessive body fat accumulation, negatively impacting health by increasing the risk of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and osteoarthritis [1,2]. Obesity is a global epidemic with an estimated prevalence of 40% among the world population, and it has been linked to the development of chronic inflammation [2]. edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Variance in the polarization of CD4 +  T cells provides more context to obesity-induced alterations of the inflammatory response. Th1 and Th17 cells have shown significant elevations in models of diet-induced obesity, along with increased production of their signature pro-inflammatory cytokines, IFNgamma , and IL-17 [10,14,46]. We have demonstrated similar results in our study, showing significantly higher polarization of Th1 and Th17 cells and significantly higher production of the IL-17 isoform IL-17A in obese mice. Our findings show enhanced Th1/Th17 polarization at postoperative day 3, which remains elevated at day 7, demonstrating a prolonged pro-inflammatory T cell response in obese mice. Additionally, we showed that leptin, a hormone derived from adipose tissue, was significantly upregulated in obese mice, which has demonstrated a pro-inflammatory role in the immune system in other studies [51,52] Leptin receptors are highly expressed on T cells, specifically Tregs, and leptin promotes T cell proliferation and increases pro-inflammatory cytokine secretion, skewing T cell polarization towards Th1/Th17 phenotypes and reducing polarization to Th2/ Treg phenotypes [14,16,46]. Tregs are essential for the suppression of the inflammatory response, as prior work suggests that Tregs are more significantly impacted by diet-induced obesity in comparison to other CD4 +  T cell subsets, producing higher levels of reactive oxygen species [16,46,53-55]. Our study demonstrated higher polarization of Tregs in obese mice prior to implantation, with indiscernible changes to the proportion of Tregs through the inflammatory time course, suggesting that the exacerbated inflammatory response associated with obesity may result from inadequate immunomodulation by Tregs.
edges.target: Immune Cell Response to Implants:Th1 cells

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Variance in the polarization of CD4 +  T cells provides more context to obesity-induced alterations of the inflammatory response. Th1 and Th17 cells have shown significant elevations in models of diet-induced obesity, along with increased production of their signature pro-inflammatory cytokines, IFNgamma , and IL-17 [10,14,46]. We have demonstrated similar results in our study, showing significantly higher polarization of Th1 and Th17 cells and significantly higher production of the IL-17 isoform IL-17A in obese mice. Our findings show enhanced Th1/Th17 polarization at postoperative day 3, which remains elevated at day 7, demonstrating a prolonged pro-inflammatory T cell response in obese mice. Additionally, we showed that leptin, a hormone derived from adipose tissue, was significantly upregulated in obese mice, which has demonstrated a pro-inflammatory role in the immune system in other studies [51,52] Leptin receptors are highly expressed on T cells, specifically Tregs, and leptin promotes T cell proliferation and increases pro-inflammatory cytokine secretion, skewing T cell polarization towards Th1/Th17 phenotypes and reducing polarization to Th2/ Treg phenotypes [14,16,46]. Tregs are essential for the suppression of the inflammatory response, as prior work suggests that Tregs are more significantly impacted by diet-induced obesity in comparison to other CD4 +  T cell subsets, producing higher levels of reactive oxygen species [16,46,53-55]. Our study demonstrated higher polarization of Tregs in obese mice prior to implantation, with indiscernible changes to the proportion of Tregs through the inflammatory time course, suggesting that the exacerbated inflammatory response associated with obesity may result from inadequate immunomodulation by Tregs.
edges.target: Immune Cell Response to Implants:Th17 cells

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Rough and rough-hydrophilic titanium (Ti) disks and implants were provided by Institut Straumann AG (Basel, Switzerland), as described previously [21,31,32].15 mm disks were fabricated from 1 mm thick unalloyed titanium grade 2. Disks were degreased in an acetone bath and treated in a 2% ammonium fluoride, 2% hydrofluoric acid, and 10% nitric acid solution to create the smooth Ti pretreatment. Rough Ti disks were fabricated by sand-blasting the pretreated smooth Ti with 0.25-0.50 mm coarse-grit corundum and acid etching in a HCl and H 2 SO 4  solution. After sand-blasting and acid etching, rough Ti disks were rinsed with deionized water and dried. Rough-hydrophilic Ti disks were produced following the same protocol to fabricate rough Ti disks but, to prevent air exposure, rinsed under a nitrogen atmosphere and sealed in an isotonic NaCl solution. 4 mm Ti implants were produced from 1 mm diameter rods and modified using the same procedure as disks. All materials were sterilized by gamma -irradiation before use.

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Scanning electron microscopy was used to qualitatively assess surface roughness (SEM, Zeiss Auriga, Carl Zeiss, Germany), and confocal microscopy was used for quantitative assessment (LSM 980 Laser Scanning Microscope, Germany) with a 20x objective and a total measurement area of 790 um x 790 um (n=3). A moving average Gaussian filter with a 30 um cut-off wavelength was used to calculate the arithmetic mean height of the scale-limited surface (Sa). Three samples were used for each surface type to calculate the mean surface roughness at six different areas. A Ram-Hart goniometer was used to measure the sessile drop contact angle to determine surface hydrophilicity (Model 100-25a, Rame-Hart Instrument Co., Succasunna, NJ). 5 uL drops of deionized water were used to measure surface hydrophilicity at six locations per sample (n=3). X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was performed using a PhI5000 VersaProbe spectrometer (ULVAC-PHI, Inc.) to determine the oxide layer composition. A focused scanning monochromatic Al-Ka source (1,486.6 eV) with a spot size of 200 um was used to acquire spectra, utilizing a base pressure of 1x10 -7  Pa. FAT analyzer mode was used to run the instrument. Detail spectra of 187.85 eV and 46.95 eV were used for the pass energy for survey scans. CasaXPS was utilized for data analysis. Shirley background subtraction was used to integrate the signals. Measurements were taken on each sample in three separate areas.

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Rough and rough-hydrophilic Ti surfaces were similar in surface roughness, both quantitatively and qualitatively (Figure 1A). Rough-hydrophilic Ti ( theta = 0deg) was more hydrophilic, and rough Ti ( theta = 93deg) was more hydrophobic, as determined by analysis of the water contact angle (Figure 1B). The wettability of rough and rough-hydrophilic Ti surfaces was correlated with the quantity of carbon in the oxide layer. Rough Ti demonstrated a higher quantity of carbon in the oxide layer than rough-hydrophilic Ti.
edges.target: Biomaterial Surface Modification:carbon content in oxide layer

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Obesity is a metabolic disease resulting from excess body fat accumulation associated with chronic systemic inflammation. Obesity has been shown to impact the function and activity of neutrophils, macrophages, and T cells, contributing to higher circulating levels of proinflammatory cytokines. Biomaterial surface properties such as roughness and hydrophilicity can influence the behavior of immune cells in the peri-implant microenvironment. This study aimed to determine how obesity induced by a high-fat diet (HFD) affects the inflammatory response to modified titanium (Ti) implants and subsequent bone formation. Obese mice had significantly more neutrophils, pro-inflammatory macrophages, and T cells and fewer anti-inflammatory macrophages and mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) in the peri-implant tissue than lean mice. Obesity also increased circulating adipokines and pro-inflammatory cytokines when compared to lean animals. Bone formation around Ti implants was reduced in obese mice compared to controls. Adoptive transfer of bone marrow cells isolated from obese mice into wild-type mice demonstrated the localized impact of obesity on immune cell function and phenotype, promoting a pro-inflammatory peri-implant microenvironment and attenuating bone formation post-implantation. These results show that obesity significantly affects the inflammatory response to modified Ti implants, prolonging the pro-inflammatory response to the implanted biomaterial and compromising bone formation.

edges.evidence_phrase: Variance in the polarization of CD4 +  T cells provides more context to obesity-induced alterations of the inflammatory response. Th1 and Th17 cells have shown significant elevations in models of diet-induced obesity, along with increased production of their signature pro-inflammatory cytokines, IFNgamma , and IL-17 [10,14,46]. We have demonstrated similar results in our study, showing significantly higher polarization of Th1 and Th17 cells and significantly higher production of the IL-17 isoform IL-17A in obese mice. Our findings show enhanced Th1/Th17 polarization at postoperative day 3, which remains elevated at day 7, demonstrating a prolonged pro-inflammatory T cell response in obese mice. Additionally, we showed that leptin, a hormone derived from adipose tissue, was significantly upregulated in obese mice, which has demonstrated a pro-inflammatory role in the immune system in other studies [51,52] Leptin receptors are highly expressed on T cells, specifically Tregs, and leptin promotes T cell proliferation and increases pro-inflammatory cytokine secretion, skewing T cell polarization towards Th1/Th17 phenotypes and reducing polarization to Th2/ Treg phenotypes [14,16,46]. Tregs are essential for the suppression of the inflammatory response, as prior work suggests that Tregs are more significantly impacted by diet-induced obesity in comparison to other CD4 +  T cell subsets, producing higher levels of reactive oxygen species [16,46,53-55]. Our study demonstrated higher polarization of Tregs in obese mice prior to implantation, with indiscernible changes to the proportion of Tregs through the inflammatory time course, suggesting that the exacerbated inflammatory response associated with obesity may result from inadequate immunomodulation by Tregs.
edges.source: Immune Cell Response to Implants:Th1 cells
edges.target: Immune Cell Response to Implants:Th17 cells

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: At 3 days post-implantation, mice receiving rough-hydrophilic Ti implants showed lower Th1 and Th17 cells and higher populations of Th2 and CD4 +  Tregs (Figure 4). Obese mice demonstrated significantly higher polarization of Th1 and Th17 subsets when compared to lean mice. Additionally, obese mice polarized less CD4 +  T cells to Th2 and Tregs. Interestingly, the polarization of Th1, Th17, and Treg subsets from obese mice was similar between mice receiving rough and rough-hydrophilic Ti implants.

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Obesity is a metabolic disease resulting from excess body fat accumulation associated with chronic systemic inflammation. Obesity has been shown to impact the function and activity of neutrophils, macrophages, and T cells, contributing to higher circulating levels of proinflammatory cytokines. Biomaterial surface properties such as roughness and hydrophilicity can influence the behavior of immune cells in the peri-implant microenvironment. This study aimed to determine how obesity induced by a high-fat diet (HFD) affects the inflammatory response to modified titanium (Ti) implants and subsequent bone formation. Obese mice had significantly more neutrophils, pro-inflammatory macrophages, and T cells and fewer anti-inflammatory macrophages and mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) in the peri-implant tissue than lean mice. Obesity also increased circulating adipokines and pro-inflammatory cytokines when compared to lean animals. Bone formation around Ti implants was reduced in obese mice compared to controls. Adoptive transfer of bone marrow cells isolated from obese mice into wild-type mice demonstrated the localized impact of obesity on immune cell function and phenotype, promoting a pro-inflammatory peri-implant microenvironment and attenuating bone formation post-implantation. These results show that obesity significantly affects the inflammatory response to modified Ti implants, prolonging the pro-inflammatory response to the implanted biomaterial and compromising bone formation.

edges.source_paper: test.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: To elucidate if the increased inflammatory phenotype observed in immune cells from obese mice persists in a normal environment, we performed an adoptive transfer of bone marrow cells from obese or lean mice into wild-type mice following Ti biomaterial implantation. Cells for adoptive transfer were isolated from the bone marrow of 26-week-old male C57BL/6J obese or lean mice. Mice were euthanized by CO 2  asphyxiation, and femur bones were removed. The bone marrow was flushed with PBS and treated with ACK buffer to remove erythrocytes (ThermoFisher Scientific). Cells were pooled and labeled with CellTracker TM  Green CMFDA Dye (ThermoFisher Scientific) for adoptive transfer experiments.
edges.source: Adoptive Transfer and Epigenetics:bone marrow cells from obese mice
edges.target: Adoptive Transfer and Epigenetics:CellTracker Green CMFDA Dye

edges.source_paper: test2.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: In vitro studies on macrophage polarization have provided additional mechanistic information. M1 macrophages are activated by exposure to LPS and inflammation-related cytokines TNFalpha or IFNgamma , alone or in combination [6,7]. They are characterized by the secretion of toxic effector molecules such as reactive oxygen species, nitric oxide, and inflammatory cytokines (IL-1 beta , IL-6, IL12, IL-23, TNFalpha ) [6]. On the other hand, M2 polarization is obtained by exposure to fungal cells, parasites, immune complexes, apoptotic cells, IL-4, IL-13, IL-10, or tumor growth factor beta (TGFbeta ) [8]. M2 macrophages are characterized by the secretion of anti-inflammatory molecules such as IL-10, TGFbeta , and glucocorticoids [9]. However, the M1/M2 paradigm does not reflect the totality of the phenotypic subsets of macrophages as defined by the activating stimuli used and can be further subdivided into M2a, M2b, M2c, M2d, and tumor-associated macrophages [10,11]. Independently of the macrophage M2 subsets, all M2 macrophages produce high levels of IL-10, which is critical in limiting the duration and intensity of immune and inflammatory responses. IL-10 inhibits the production of IL-1 beta , IL-6, and TNFalpha in macrophages by downregulating Major Histocompatibility Complex II (MHC II) and co-stimulatory molecules [10].

edges.source_paper: test2.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Macrophages play a central role in orchestrating the inflammatory response to implanted biomaterials and are sensitive to changes in the chemical and physical characteristics of the implant. Macrophages respond to biological, chemical, and physical cues by polarizing into pro-inflammatory (M1) or anti-inflammatory (M2) states. We previously showed that roughhydrophilic titanium (Ti) implants skew macrophage polarization towards an anti-inflammatory phenotype and increase mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) recruitment and bone formation around the implant. In the present study, we aimed to investigate whether the adoptive transfer of macrophages in different polarization states would alter the inflammatory microenvironment and improve biomaterial integration in macrophage-competent and macrophage-ablated mice. We found that ablating macrophages increased the presence of neutrophils, reduced T cells and MSCs, and compromised the healing and biomaterial integration process. These effects could not be rescued with adoptive transfer of nave or polarized macrophages. Adoptive transfer of M1 macrophages into macrophage-competent mice increased inflammatory cells and inflammatory microenvironment, resulting in decreased bone-to-implant contact. Adoptive transfer of M2 macrophages into macrophage-competent mice reduced the pro-inflammatory environment in the peri-implant tissue and increased bone-to-implant contact. Taken together, our results show the importance of macrophages in controlling and modulating the inflammatory process in response to implanted biomaterials and suggest they can be used to improve outcomes following biomaterial implantation.

edges.source_paper: test2.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: In vitro studies on macrophage polarization have provided additional mechanistic information. M1 macrophages are activated by exposure to LPS and inflammation-related cytokines TNFalpha or IFNgamma , alone or in combination [6,7]. They are characterized by the secretion of toxic effector molecules such as reactive oxygen species, nitric oxide, and inflammatory cytokines (IL-1 beta , IL-6, IL12, IL-23, TNFalpha ) [6]. On the other hand, M2 polarization is obtained by exposure to fungal cells, parasites, immune complexes, apoptotic cells, IL-4, IL-13, IL-10, or tumor growth factor beta (TGFbeta ) [8]. M2 macrophages are characterized by the secretion of anti-inflammatory molecules such as IL-10, TGFbeta , and glucocorticoids [9]. However, the M1/M2 paradigm does not reflect the totality of the phenotypic subsets of macrophages as defined by the activating stimuli used and can be further subdivided into M2a, M2b, M2c, M2d, and tumor-associated macrophages [10,11]. Independently of the macrophage M2 subsets, all M2 macrophages produce high levels of IL-10, which is critical in limiting the duration and intensity of immune and inflammatory responses. IL-10 inhibits the production of IL-1 beta , IL-6, and TNFalpha in macrophages by downregulating Major Histocompatibility Complex II (MHC II) and co-stimulatory molecules [10].

edges.source_paper: test2.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Macrophages play a central role in orchestrating the inflammatory response to implanted biomaterials and are sensitive to changes in the chemical and physical characteristics of the implant. Macrophages respond to biological, chemical, and physical cues by polarizing into pro-inflammatory (M1) or anti-inflammatory (M2) states. We previously showed that roughhydrophilic titanium (Ti) implants skew macrophage polarization towards an anti-inflammatory phenotype and increase mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) recruitment and bone formation around the implant. In the present study, we aimed to investigate whether the adoptive transfer of macrophages in different polarization states would alter the inflammatory microenvironment and improve biomaterial integration in macrophage-competent and macrophage-ablated mice. We found that ablating macrophages increased the presence of neutrophils, reduced T cells and MSCs, and compromised the healing and biomaterial integration process. These effects could not be rescued with adoptive transfer of nave or polarized macrophages. Adoptive transfer of M1 macrophages into macrophage-competent mice increased inflammatory cells and inflammatory microenvironment, resulting in decreased bone-to-implant contact. Adoptive transfer of M2 macrophages into macrophage-competent mice reduced the pro-inflammatory environment in the peri-implant tissue and increased bone-to-implant contact. Taken together, our results show the importance of macrophages in controlling and modulating the inflammatory process in response to implanted biomaterials and suggest they can be used to improve outcomes following biomaterial implantation.

edges.evidence_phrase: In vitro studies on macrophage polarization have provided additional mechanistic information. M1 macrophages are activated by exposure to LPS and inflammation-related cytokines TNFalpha or IFNgamma , alone or in combination [6,7]. They are characterized by the secretion of toxic effector molecules such as reactive oxygen species, nitric oxide, and inflammatory cytokines (IL-1 beta , IL-6, IL12, IL-23, TNFalpha ) [6]. On the other hand, M2 polarization is obtained by exposure to fungal cells, parasites, immune complexes, apoptotic cells, IL-4, IL-13, IL-10, or tumor growth factor beta (TGFbeta ) [8]. M2 macrophages are characterized by the secretion of anti-inflammatory molecules such as IL-10, TGFbeta , and glucocorticoids [9]. However, the M1/M2 paradigm does not reflect the totality of the phenotypic subsets of macrophages as defined by the activating stimuli used and can be further subdivided into M2a, M2b, M2c, M2d, and tumor-associated macrophages [10,11]. Independently of the macrophage M2 subsets, all M2 macrophages produce high levels of IL-10, which is critical in limiting the duration and intensity of immune and inflammatory responses. IL-10 inhibits the production of IL-1 beta , IL-6, and TNFalpha in macrophages by downregulating Major Histocompatibility Complex II (MHC II) and co-stimulatory molecules [10].

However, the M1/M2 paradigm does not reflect the totality of the phenotypic subsets of macrophages as defined by the activating stimuli used and can be further subdivided into M2a, M2b, M2c, M2d, and tumor-associated macrophages [10,11]. Independently of the macrophage M2 subsets, all M2 macrophages produce high levels of IL-10, which is critical in limiting the duration and intensity of immune and inflammatory responses. IL-10 inhibits the production of IL-1 beta , IL-6, and TNFalpha in macrophages by downregulating Major Histocompatibility Complex II (MHC II) and co-stimulatory molecules [10].

Independently of the macrophage M2 subsets, all M2 macrophages produce high levels of IL-10, which is critical in limiting the duration and intensity of immune and inflammatory responses. IL-10 inhibits the production of IL-1 beta , IL-6, and TNFalpha in macrophages by downregulating Major Histocompatibility Complex II (MHC II) and co-stimulatory molecules [10].

edges.source_paper: test2.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Macrophages play a significant role in the inflammatory response following biomaterial implantation, eliminating cellular and tissue debris from the surgical procedure, modulating the inflammatory milieu by regulating cytokine and chemokine release, and recruiting other immune cells. The physicochemical properties of the surface of the implanted biomaterial have been shown to alter macrophage activation and phenotype [22-25]. Our group has demonstrated that macrophages cultured on rough-hydrophilic titanium (Ti) substrates preferentially activate into an M2-like phenotype with higher arginase expression and IL-10 secretion compared to macrophages cultured on hydrophobic Ti substrates, which preferentially activate macrophages into a M1-like phenotype with high secretion of IL-1 beta , IL-6, and TNFalpha , and high expression of nitric oxide synthase (Nos2) [2426]. Importantly, these effects occur in vitro, where damage-associated molecular patterns (DAMPs), cytokines, and lipid inflammatory mediators from surrounding tissues and cells are absent [22-24].

edges.evidence_phrase: Fc receptors were blocked in peri-implant tissue single-cell suspensions by incubation with TruStain FcX Plus (BioLegend, San Diego, CA) to prevent non-specific antibody binding. A fixable viability dye was used to exclude dead cells (Zombie NIR, BioLegend). Cells were then incubated with fluorescent antibodies to identify neutrophils (CD45 + /CD11b + /Ly6C + /Ly6G + ), macrophages (CD45 + /CD11b + / MHCII + /Cd11c - /F40/80 + ), pro-inflammatory macrophages (CD45 + /CD11b + /MHCII + / Cd11c - /F40/80 + /CD206 - /CD80 + ), anti-inflammatory macrophages (CD45 + /CD11b + / MHCII + /Cd11c - /F40/80 + /CD80 - /CD206 + ), T cells (CD45 + /CD11b - /CD3 + ), T helper cells (CD45 + /CD11b - /CD3 + /CD8 - /CD4 + ), cytotoxic T cells (CD45 + /CD11b - /CD3 + /CD4 - / CD8 + ), natural killer T (NKT) cells (CD45 + /CD11b - /CD3 + /CD4 - /CD8 - /NK1.1 + ) and mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs, CD45 - /Sca1 + /CD90 + /CD105 + ) (BioLegend). Samples were analyzed by spectral flow cytometry using a Cytek Aurora CS instrument (Cytek Biosciences, Fremont, CA), and 200,000 events were collected per sample. Single color and fluorescence minus one control were used to establish the gating strategy, as previously reported [30]. Results were analyzed using FlowJo software (FlowJo LLC, Ashland, OR).

edges.source_paper: test2.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Macrophages play a central role in orchestrating the inflammatory response to implanted biomaterials and are sensitive to changes in the chemical and physical characteristics of the implant. Macrophages respond to biological, chemical, and physical cues by polarizing into pro-inflammatory (M1) or anti-inflammatory (M2) states. We previously showed that roughhydrophilic titanium (Ti) implants skew macrophage polarization towards an anti-inflammatory phenotype and increase mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) recruitment and bone formation around the implant. In the present study, we aimed to investigate whether the adoptive transfer of macrophages in different polarization states would alter the inflammatory microenvironment and improve biomaterial integration in macrophage-competent and macrophage-ablated mice. We found that ablating macrophages increased the presence of neutrophils, reduced T cells and MSCs, and compromised the healing and biomaterial integration process. These effects could not be rescued with adoptive transfer of nave or polarized macrophages. Adoptive transfer of M1 macrophages into macrophage-competent mice increased inflammatory cells and inflammatory microenvironment, resulting in decreased bone-to-implant contact. Adoptive transfer of M2 macrophages into macrophage-competent mice reduced the pro-inflammatory environment in the peri-implant tissue and increased bone-to-implant contact. Taken together, our results show the importance of macrophages in controlling and modulating the inflammatory process in response to implanted biomaterials and suggest they can be used to improve outcomes following biomaterial implantation.
edges.source: Biomaterial Surface Properties and Immune Response:rough-hydrophilic titanium (Ti) implants

edges.source_paper: test2.pdf
edges.evidence_phrase: Surface-modified Ti implants (Institut Straumann, Basel, Switzerland) were fabricated from 1 mm unalloyed titanium grade 5 rods as previously described [30-32]. Titanium implants (length=4 mm; =1mm) were degreased in an acetone bath and treated with 2% ammonium fluoride, 2% hydrofluoric acid, and 10% nitric acid to produce smooth Ti implants. Smooth Ti implants were sand-blasted (0.25-0.50 mm coarse-grit corundum) and acid-etched (HCl and H2 SO 4 ) to produce rough implants. After processing, smooth and rough Ti implants were rinsed in deionized water and dried on the benchtop. To create super-hydrophilic implants, Ti rods were rinsed under a nitrogen atmosphere after sandblasting and acid etching and stored in an isotonic NaCl solution to prevent atmospheric contamination. Implants were sterilized by gamma -irradiation before use.
edges.source: Biomaterial Surface Properties and Immune Response:smooth Ti implants
edges.target: Biomaterial Surface Properties and Immune Response:rough Ti implants
